rationale: The page contains a world map detailing recent natural disasters including volcanic eruptions, earthquakes, cyclones, and floods. This information is highly relevant for environmental geography and current affairs.
cleanedText:
7.2.3. DISASTER AFFECTED AREAS IN NEWS
World: Disaster Affected Areas in News
- Mount Etna (Italy): Volcanic Eruption. Located in Sicily Island, Italy. Europe's largest active volcano and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
- Türkiye (Capital: Ankara): Earthquake hits Turkey's Balikesir Province.
- Cyclone Asna: Tropical cyclone along the west coast, Arabian Sea.
- Vietnam (Capital: Hanoi): Typhoon Bualoi in the Northwest Pacific Ocean.
- Peru (Capital: Lima): Earthquake strike the coast of southern Peru.
- Chile (Capital: Santiago): 7.4 magnitude earthquake hits Southern Chile.
- Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) (Capital: Kinshasa): Floods occurred in eastern part of DRC near shores of Lake Tanganyika.
- Afghanistan (Capital: Kabul): Earthquake in Kunar Province.
- Algeria (Capital: Algiers): Earthquake strikes Tebessa province.
- Mount Lewotobi Laki-laki (Indonesia): Volcanic Eruption. Located in Flores Island, Indonesia.
- Kilauea volcano (Hawaii, U.S.): Volcanic Eruption. Located on the Southeastern part of the island of Hawaii.
- Hayli Gubbi (Ethiopia): Volcanic Eruption sent a massive ash cloud across Red Sea and South Asia.
- Mount Semeru (Indonesia): Volcanic Eruption. Stratovolcano; Highest volcano on Java Island; part of the Pacific Ring of Fire.
- Merapi Volcano: Located in Indonesia.
